name: Vietnamese Village Hut
prompt:
Traditional Vietnamese village hut (nha san) - stilted house found in Mekong Delta and Central Highlands villages.

Physical structure:
- Stilts: 4-6 wooden poles/posts raising the floor ~1.5m above ground
  - Rough-hewn timber, ~0.15m diameter
  - Slightly tapered, driven into ground
- Floor: rectangular wooden plank platform ~4m x 3m
  - Bamboo mat/plank floor
- Walls: woven bamboo/thatch walls on 3 sides
  - Walls ~2m tall from floor
  - Bamboo frame visible at edges
  - Front wall: open or with simple woven panel door
- Roof: steep thatched palm leaf roof
  - A-frame style, high peaked ~45 degree angle
  - Ridge line runs along length
  - Thatch overhangs walls by ~0.5m
  - Color is golden-brown dried palm
- Ladder: simple bamboo ladder from ground to floor platform at entrance
  - 4-5 rungs
- Cross-bracing: diagonal supports between stilts

Colors: Aged wood stilts (0x8B7355), bamboo walls (0xb5a068), golden thatch roof (0xc8a850), darker thatch (0xa88830), bamboo floor (0xbfa858)
Orientation: Entrance facing +Z, stilts on ground at Y=0
Scale: Real-world meters (~4m long, ~3m wide, ~4m total height)
